text:
User: use inquirer crate -- make it called `tenex-edge config` -- one of the menu options is configuring providers, another is configuring models (with model listing from the different providers (i.e. list the models from the configured ollama endpoint (somtimes localhost, could be anything, could be sometimes ollama cloud, etc) -- or the openrouter models -- with fuzzy search, etc a REAL good UX [...] while you start working on the basics launch a fable agent to give you the UX/UI (including colors, etc) so we don't make something shitty
 [...] The Fable agent's UX spec is at `/private/tmp/claude-501/.../scratchpad/tenex-edge-config-ux-spec.md` (478 lines). I applied its highest-value calls: the color palette, the merged provider-list-with-actions pattern (vs. a separate Add/Edit/Remove menu), Test connection, correct secret masking, skip-trivial-choice, and the "no providers → offer to set one up" bridge. Not yet applied (deferred as lower-priority polish, not blocking): the confirm screen's `was: <old value>` diff line, OSC-11 dark/light auto-detection, the braille spinner (currently a plain dimmed line), and response caching for OpenRouter's 300+ model fetch within a session — happy to do any of these next if you want the full spec realized. [...] User: Another Claude session sent a message:
<teammate-message teammate_id="fable-ux" color="blue" summary="Config UX spec delivered with palette + key decisions">
Full spec written to /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-pablofernandez-src-tenex-edge/026d1502-e769-4a9c-9ab5-26169a4150ef/scratchpad/tenex-edge-config-ux-spec.md — palette, all screens with exact copy and ASCII mockups, fuzzy-row anatomy, iconography with ASCII degrade, confirm-before-write pattern, and inquire RenderConfig mapping notes.

## Palette (ANSI-256 / hex)

| Role | Dark theme | Light theme |
|---|---|---|
| Accent (prompt ?, pointer, answers) | 45 #00d7ff | 31 #0087af |
| Success | 78 #5fd787 | 29 #00875f |
| Error | 203 #ff5f5f | 160 #d70000 |
| Warning | 214 #ffaf00 | 130 #af5f00 |
| Muted (help, metadata, paths) | 245 #8a8a8a | 242 #6c6c6c |
| Disabled | 240 #585858 | 249 #b2b2b2 |
| Fuzzy-match substring | accent + bold, NO background fill | same |
| Selected row | accent ❯ pointer; row text default-fg + bold | same |

Theme selection: OSC 11 background query (terminal-light crate), fallback to dark palette (its colors were chosen to stay ≥3:1 on white too), TENEX_EDGE_THEME override, NO_COLOR honored. Cyan accent matches the existing ratatui picker. No emoji; ASCII degrade (>, ok:, error:, -\|/) when locale isn't UTF-8.

## Three decisions not to get wrong

1. **Fuzzy rows are single-line, two-zone**: primary text (matched chars accent+bold) + dimmed metadata suffix in an aligned column. OpenRouter primary is the **id** (not display name — that's what lands in llms.json), metadata `200k ctx · $3.00/$15.00 per Mtok`; Ollama is name + `4.7 GB · 2 days ago`, sorted modified-desc. Never use a background color for match highlight (unknown-theme hazard) and never wrap rows; drop price <100 cols, all metadata <70. Page size 12 with a live `3/312` counter.

2. **Errors recover in place, never dead-end**: every fetch failure is one `✗ what/where/why` line plus a Retry / Edit-provider / Back select that inlines the fix and auto-retries. Includes the "reachable but not actually Ollama" case. Empty-provider entry to Models offers to jump straight into add-provider; after adding a provider, offer to assign a role immediately.

3. **Confirm-before-write is a semantic summary, not a JSON diff**: `edge-distillation → qwen2.5:7b (ollama)  was: mistral:7b`, default Yes; answering No returns to the previous prompt with state intact. Atomic writes, unknown JSON keys round-tripped, secrets always shown truncated (`sk-or-…4f2a`) everywhere including receipts and errors. Removing a provider warns which roles break but leaves them visibly dangling rather than auto-deleting.
</teammate-message> [...] Full spec written to /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-pablofernandez-src-tenex-edge/026d1502-e769-4a9c-9ab5-26169a4150ef/scratchpad/tenex-edge-config-ux-spec.md — palette, all screens with exact copy and ASCII mockups, fuzzy-row anatomy, iconography with ASCII degrade, confirm-before-write pattern, and inquire RenderConfig mapping notes.
